bullets.text: Ukrainian drones have targeted St Petersburg in an attack Russia has called 'unprecedented,' urging residents of its second-largest city to stay indoors for the first time since the war began.
The US and Iran have exchanged strikes in the Gulf, with the US military targeting Iranian drones and radar sites, while Tehran claims to have targeted US bases in Kuwait and Bahrain.
US health officials warn that the spread of Ebola in central Africa could reach the record levels of the 2014 outbreak, with modelling showing a 'dangerous trajectory.'
Tasmania's police minister, Felix Ellis, has apologised for naming the Port Arthur gunman during budget estimates, with a survivor of the 1996 massacre describing his comments as "hurtful."
Australia is experiencing its winter illness season, with health officials monitoring prevalent viruses after a record influenza season in 2025.
The Queensland government faces accusations of operating a "secret agenda" to reduce Indigenous representation in the public sector, with a barrister describing it as an "organised strategy."
The European Central Bank is expected to lead global interest rate tightening with a hike in the coming week, driven by the ongoing Iran war.
Oil tanker owners are concerned about a potential market crash and a steep drop in rates if the Strait of Hormuz reopens, after investing windfall profits into new vessels during the Iran war.
Federal Reserve Governor Michael Barr has critici zed recent proposals to relax rules for US lenders, arguing that these changes would considerably weaken bank regulation and supervision.
